110297.explanation: Aldermen should be elected based on their qualities. Both men and women are eligible.

110298.explanation: Participation of residents, businesses, and associations is extremely important. A referendum, like citizen councils, is a good means for this.

110302.explanation: Incentive subsidies to promote sustainability, awareness, and cooperation are a good instrument.

110304.explanation: Provided that the weather (sufficient wind) permits it with regard to public health.

110306.explanation: Fireworks are a tradition in the celebration of New Year’s Eve. To honor that tradition and reduce the chance of illegal fireworks, a fireworks show helps.

110307.explanation: We adhere to the principle that the polluter pays.

110310.explanation: The municipality must encourage welfare beneficiaries and especially their children to join a sports or cultural association. This strengthens their mental and physical health and enhances their social network. If a financial contribution is needed, the municipality should contribute to that.

110314.explanation: Our municipality has more than 800 monuments, and besides the people, these largely define who we are as a municipality. We must cherish that!

110316.explanation: For a safer Bergen op Zoom, this is unfortunately a necessity, also in the outskirts. Especially more mobile cameras.

110318.explanation: Where aggression increases, stronger means for intervention and protection are needed.

110324.explanation: Everyone counts and participates. A reciprocal obligation for benefits is meaningful to show people and help them experience that they matter.

110326.explanation: Bergen op Zoom has a generous income limit. We must ensure that everyone entitled to assistance is actually reached.

110327.explanation: Current times show that we need to invest more in youth work. Investing to reach into the very roots of all our neighborhoods and improve livability for all residents.

110328.explanation: Where there is a need, the municipality should designate more charging points.

110329.explanation: Three reasons: clarity for road traffic on all roads within the built-up area; more traffic safety; more space for cyclists, pedestrians, and greenery.

110330.explanation: Where more regulation is needed, this can be a good instrument. This requires prior consultation with the residents and entrepreneurs of the neighborhood.

110333.explanation: The municipality should mainly focus on quality, results, and sustainability.

110337.explanation: This was only recently aligned with comparable municipalities.

110338.explanation: When it comes to the development of new business parks, we see this as a regional issue. For necessary expansion, we prefer to cooperate with Steenbergen and Woensdrecht.

110340.explanation: You can only take care of new residents if you first take good care of your own residents. No priority for refugees as long as our own residents have to wait an extremely long time for their own home.

110342.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and absolutely necessary for food production. Therefore, no solar panels on land, but on roofs.

110344.explanation: Parents have their own responsibility. The municipality does have a stimulating role.

110347.explanation: We must build what fits and is needed. This may differ per neighborhood or village. The number of social rental homes is a derivative of that and not a strict rule. The target of 30% social rental homes is a guideline for us, but not an end in itself.

110348.explanation: Only where it is possible and fits in the environment. This way we also ensure more space for greenery.

110349.explanation: That depends on the target group to whom the rental is made, for example, room rentals for students.

110356.explanation: Depends on costs, external subsidies, and the feasibility of the Westersingel plan.

110357.explanation: GBWP is against large-scale housing of labor migrants. The Havendijk is an A-location by the water and therefore much better suited for adjacent business activities.

110297.explanation: Having an ambition is of course nice, but we primarily focus on quality.

110298.explanation: A referendum does not fit with our representative democracy. Moreover, referendum propositions lack the necessary nuances.

110302.explanation: The municipality should facilitate initiatives and speed up procedures, but not provide structural subsidies. Sustainability must remain affordable.

110304.explanation: We attach great value to individual freedoms.

110306.explanation: The municipality should focus on safety and enforcement. A fireworks show is not a core task. However, facilitating such an initiative with as few rules as possible is welcomed.

110307.explanation: We want to facilitate the residents of Bergen as much as possible regarding waste.

110310.explanation: Sport promotes health and participation. The municipality can provide discounts or contributions for memberships so that participation is feasible for everyone. For children, we will pay anyway. We want people to first turn to third parties like the Leergeld foundation.

110314.explanation: Monuments contribute to the livability and recognizability of Bergen op Zoom. Good maintenance prevents decay and keeps the city attractive to live in and visit.

110316.explanation: Camera surveillance works, especially at hotspots.

110318.explanation: BOAs face more aggression and serious crime than before. They must be able to defend themselves to effectively tackle criminals.

110324.explanation: Participation according to capacity belongs to receiving benefits.

110326.explanation: The income threshold is fine as it currently is.

110327.explanation: Youth work as currently performed focuses far too much on certain neighborhoods and groups. This particularly applies to the JOC. We want to make things easier for all youth through housing, nightlife, and education policies.

110328.explanation: Bergen is a frontrunner when it comes to charging points. The VVD focuses on cable ducts to enable people to charge their cars from home.

110329.explanation: We want to keep the 30 km/h limit only inside neighborhoods. On main roads within the built-up area, this causes a false sense of safety and poor traffic flow.

110330.explanation: Paid parking only where necessary to manage available spaces. Additional rules do not resolve parking pressure.

110333.explanation: We must comply with the legal obligation. Within that framework, we must look at costs. Expenditures on youth care are rising unreasonably.

110334.explanation: We already take care of this here.

110337.explanation: We want to remain attractive to visitors.

110338.explanation: Employment is crucial for our municipality. We are already lagging behind. The VVD wants to develop the Auvergnepolder as soon as possible.

110340.explanation: No priority above local residents. The housing market is already tight enough.

110342.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs and industrial sites.

110344.explanation: School swimming promotes safety and health among children and must be accessible to everyone. Schools and affluent school boards should be the first to provide funding.

110347.explanation: Part of the lower segment may also consist of cheap owner-occupied homes and private affordable rentals, as long as the housing mix remains affordable and balanced for starters and local residents.

110348.explanation: High is relative. Building for youth and starters lends itself excellently to building upwards. We should show ambition and seize opportunities.

110349.explanation: Room rental is allowed, provided there are strict rules and rents remain fair and affordable. This means no rooming for labor migrants.

110354.explanation: There is already a night shelter. Ideally, this should be relocated elsewhere in the region, for example to Roosendaal.

110356.explanation: Water and a bridge for the Gevangenpoort make no historical sense. It is an expensive plan that contributes little to the city; the investment does not match the utility or experience.

110357.explanation: Building at the Havendijk is possible, and social safety is well guaranteed. We choose the edges of industrial areas, primarily to return family homes in neighborhoods to those for whom they are intended.

110297.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, for example. In such a government, all perspectives are represented.

110298.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

110302.explanation: We believe that cooperative initiatives by residents in this area deserve support from the municipality. In this way, we stimulate the energy transition and ensure lower energy costs for our residents.

110304.explanation: We stand for cleaner air and healthier air quality for our residents. To achieve this, we want to work with residents to explore what measures we can take in Bergen op Zoom. We believe more in promoting the use of sustainable options and less in prohibiting.

110306.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all celebrate together, with one big fireworks show organized by the municipality with input from residents. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year's Eve together.

110307.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

110310.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

110314.explanation: Our monuments make our municipality beautiful and unique. We believe they need proper care. It would be a great shame if unique places are lost forever because we choose to cut budgets now.

110316.explanation: We see safety as a top priority for the coming years. To keep our neighborhoods safe and livable, we want each neighborhood to have a safety plan developed in consultation with residents. This should include more camera surveillance, of course respecting the privacy of our citizens.

110318.explanation: We believe that community enforcement officers (boa's) must always be able to do their work safely. We think that boa's currently already have enough tools to perform their duties well. In consultation with the boa's, we will continue to assess whether more instruments are needed in the future.

110324.explanation: Most people are not on social assistance because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra requirements and conditions actually create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

110326.explanation: During the 2022 energy crisis, the municipality raised the income limit on our initiative, allowing more residents to use the energy fund to pay their bills. We believe the income limit should be raised permanently to help more residents of Bergen.

110327.explanation: Young people face pressures from all sides, from unaffordable housing to addictive social media algorithms. With youth work, we prevent young people from (further) getting into trouble. We also contribute to safe and strong neighborhoods where it is nice to live and grow up.

110328.explanation: We see that more residents are driving electric vehicles. To facilitate and encourage this, we think it makes sense to designate more parking spaces as public charging points. We will ensure this does not come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

110329.explanation: The data clearly shows: lower maximum speeds make traffic in our municipality MUCH safer. For cycling children and elderly with limited mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the air becomes cleaner.

110330.explanation: We do not support introducing a parking permit system in more neighborhoods. If parking pressure is too high, we will consult with residents about solutions, where stricter enforcement and paid parking can be options.

110333.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and care are enormous. You cannot solve this by cutting budgets further or always choosing the cheapest option. We must primarily ensure certainty and quality, offering help that matches the concerns of our youth.

110334.explanation: In recent years, a lot has already been done here on our initiative in the municipality. Free menstrual products are now distributed in more locations. In the coming years, we will focus on even more distribution points for menstrual products.

110337.explanation: We are against raising the tourist tax. We want to keep Bergen op Zoom attractive for tourism. Furthermore, many entrepreneurs already face a significant VAT increase on overnight stays from the government.

110338.explanation: We support more space for businesses and employment. We focus on qualitative and high-quality businesses that create jobs. We also take into account historical and landscape values, such as in the Auvergnepolder.

110340.explanation: Bergen op Zoom faces multiple priority groups, of which status holders are one. They cannot build their lives from shelters, which also blocks the asylum chain. Ultimately, the solution is to build more affordable housing for everyone.

110342.explanation: A solar panel field can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want to, they should be able to use their land for this. However, we believe that priority should first be given to solar panels on roofs.

110344.explanation: Bergen op Zoom is a water-rich municipality and every child should learn to swim here, including those whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution is school swimming for every child, supported by the municipality.

110347.explanation: Every party wants to build homes. But the real question is: which party wants to build more affordable homes? We do! That is why we support at least 30% social housing and at least 50% mid-range housing, while monitoring housing prices. This way, everyone can find a nice home.

110348.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. With high-rise buildings, you can quickly add many homes. We say: build upwards near good public transport connections. This makes good use of valuable land while taking into account neighbors and the surroundings.

110349.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality have one or more spare rooms. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well maintained and rented at a fair price.

110354.explanation: Under the leadership of our alderman van Aken, we have already seen an increase in the number of beds in the night shelter for homeless people in Bergen op Zoom in recent years. In the coming years, we want to expand this because we believe no one should sleep on the streets in Bergen op Zoom.

110356.explanation: At the Westersingel, we first give (financial) priority to more affordable housing and a safe traffic situation. We respect the historical and monumental values there, but realizing the water and the bridge is not our priority.

110357.explanation: Currently, housing for migrant workers is insufficient. Migrant workers often live in degrading conditions, and we want to change that. Therefore, building at the Havendijk is very important so that migrant workers have decent and humane housing.

110298.explanation: Indeed about important matters. At least two-thirds of the municipal council must agree upon importance evaluation.

110302.explanation: Permit should be granted free of charge. Financial support is not a municipal task.

110304.explanation: Of course, it must be safe!

110306.explanation: Continue to encourage this through associations. Limited allowance for private individuals should be possible.

110307.explanation: Definitely. Otherwise, people will resort to illegal dumping.

110310.explanation: Only through the social assistance benefit.

110314.explanation: Selective. National monuments are the responsibility of the state. Otherwise only what is very characteristic for Bergen op Zoom. Co-financing with the initiator.

110316.explanation: Originally started by Lijst Linssen with own initiative and investment! So yes.

110318.explanation: Unfortunately necessary at this time. It must be safe for the boa's.

110324.explanation: Also better for the benefit recipient. It provides more incentive and chance to find work sooner.

110326.explanation: State responsibility and if funds are available.

110327.explanation: Unfortunately, that is not possible. The data indicates it is urgently needed. So unwise.

110328.explanation: Only where possible. It's still really too early. Many people would be disadvantaged now. Possibly in the near future.

110329.explanation: With the exception of the main arteries!

110330.explanation: Costs again for the citizen. Unnecessary.

110333.explanation: Must mainly focus on quality and reviews.

110334.explanation: No municipal responsibility. Or through social assistance benefits.

110338.explanation: Businesses can be established in the Auvergnepolder.

110340.explanation: Absolutely not! Our own residents, who have been on the waiting list for a very long time, come first.

110342.explanation: No. Only on roofs in industrial areas. That is wasted space and visually not disturbing.

110344.explanation: Important for the health of our youth.

110347.explanation: Always align with market needs. Locally. Don’t set standards. The housing market remains unpredictable.

110348.explanation: It's about the price. No fundamental objections. Naturally near the city or village center.

110349.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, not too many obstacles.

110354.explanation: Homeless shelters cause a lot of nuisance. The facility attracts people. Remains a difficult topic. If absolutely necessary (?), near the police station and combined with supervision.

110356.explanation: Only if financially feasible. But then the Kaai should have the preference.

110357.explanation: Preferably realize this near workplaces. And preferably have the employers do it.

110297.explanation: The goal must definitely be that the council reflects society; this also includes a good male/female balance.

110298.explanation: A referendum can be a good tool on certain topics for direct involvement in your own community. Additionally, we want to work on a participatory budgeting process.

110302.explanation: We should help and guide residents who want to jointly generate electricity through, for example, an energy cooperative. The financing is something the initiators must arrange themselves, as with any party that generates energy.

110304.explanation: If you have an open fireplace or wood stove, you should be able to continue using it. However, we want to align with the national burning guide and prohibit wood burning during fog or calm weather (red code).

110306.explanation: We do want to facilitate residents who want to organize a fireworks show together. Organizing a fireworks show is not seen as a municipal task.

110307.explanation: Waste processing costs money; the current method where Saver collects your bulky waste after a few ticks fits this.

110310.explanation: Sports promote health and help you socialize. Residents with low incomes can benefit greatly from this, so we must ensure everyone has access to sports. A free membership at a club can help.

110314.explanation: Bergen op Zoom is rich in history and therefore monuments. We must maintain them well. Poor maintenance leads to much higher costs in the future.

110316.explanation: Temporary deployment for nuisance can be effective, but we do not support permanent camera surveillance. That is too great an invasion of privacy.

110318.explanation: Our enforcement officers are effective with current resources. The approach is mainly personal contact and prevention. Weapons belong to the police.

110324.explanation: Social assistance should not focus on delivering a counterperformance, but on developing people; a counterperformance can help with that. The municipality should proactively approach people on social assistance, assess their needs, and find ways to help them sustainably into work.

110326.explanation: The income limit used by Bergen op Zoom is fine. However, we must do more to reach residents entitled to poverty regulations.

110327.explanation: Youth work is an important link in reducing nuisance and promoting connection.

110328.explanation: We need to install more charging stations, especially fast chargers, to support the transition to electric driving.

110329.explanation: 30 km/h is better for safety and hardly affects travel time within built-up areas.

110330.explanation: By introducing parking permits, we limit parking nuisance and keep more parking spaces available for neighborhood residents.

110333.explanation: The quality of youth care is more important and can ultimately save costs by sustainably helping young people.

110334.explanation: Poverty should never mean women lack tampons or sanitary pads. We see these as basic needs, and we must help with that. Fortunately, there are already several menstrual product distribution points in Bergen op Zoom, which we will continue to support.

110337.explanation: At this time, we do not see a need to increase the tourist tax. Bergen op Zoom should actually attract more tourism. We do support annual indexation in line with inflation.

110338.explanation: We have a shortage of business parks, so we need to develop more and cooperate with other municipalities in the Brabantse Wal region.

110340.explanation: Blaming one group for the housing shortage is no solution; abolishing the priority rule solves nothing. More important is that we build enough houses. Our ambition is to build 7,000 homes by 2040.

110342.explanation: Solar panels on roofs are our first preference, but we also find it acceptable that a farmer leases his land for a number of years for solar panels. It is good for the energy transition and beneficial to the land which then gets some rest.

110344.explanation: Unfortunately, we cannot facilitate everything as a municipality. Financing school swimming is a task for the national government.

110347.explanation: In all new construction plans, we have agreed to build 30% social/affordable housing. We want to stick to this agreement for the 7,000 homes we aim to build by 2040.

110348.explanation: To achieve our ambition of building 7,000 homes by 2040, we will need high-rise buildings. This is also appropriate in and around plein 13.

110349.explanation: We must properly regulate individual room rentals, but room rental can be a solution for the housing shortage. Youth housing could be well accommodated via rooms. We also want to work on splitting homes.

110354.explanation: We currently have nuisance from homeless people who have nowhere to go. A roof over your head is a necessity from a humanitarian perspective. Structural shelter must be spread across the region, but Bergen op Zoom will always have homeless people who need a place to sleep.

110356.explanation: The current layout does justice neither to the Gevangenpoort nor to the connection between the Harbor Quarter and the city center. By restoring the water and the bridge, we create a valuable connection that better showcases our heritage.

110357.explanation: Many migrant workers in our region work in agriculture and logistics. They have the right to proper housing. This complex will reduce nuisance and overcrowding in the neighborhoods, which we want to strictly enforce.

110297.explanation: We value balance. That is why our own candidate list has an equal number of men and women. But “must” means enforcing with a quota: that goes too far.

110298.explanation: Referendums give residents a clear voice and make governance transparent. By the way, that is already possible in Bergen op Zoom.

110302.explanation: Sustainable energy is fine, but not at the expense of others. Subsidies come from taxpayers’ money. We choose fairness and affordability for everyone.

110304.explanation: A ban barely helps against air pollution: the vast majority comes from Antwerp. What is important is that people burn wood properly and consider each other.

110306.explanation: A fireworks show is festive but costs a lot of taxpayers’ money. First, see if entrepreneurs can organize this. They can do it better than the municipality. Together we’ll make it a great celebration.

110307.explanation: Free bulky waste pickup makes it accessible and prevents illegal dumping. That is cheaper and ensures clean, livable neighborhoods.

110310.explanation: There is already targeted support for low-income households through existing schemes, often also through clubs themselves. Extra taxpayer money is not needed; keep it fair and affordable.

110314.explanation: We are proud of our monuments. The budget is already very modest but keeps our history visible and the identity of the municipality strong.

110316.explanation: More camera surveillance helps tackle nuisance and crime. It provides safety and supports enforcers and the police.

110318.explanation: Public order officers must be able to defend themselves but are not police. A baton can help but can also escalate. Careful use and training are important.

110324.explanation: Everyone contributes according to their ability. That makes our society stronger and more cohesive and increases the chance of work and participation.

110326.explanation: Poverty also affects middle incomes burdened with high expenses. Groceries are getting more expensive and fixed costs higher. More attention to household budgets is needed.

110327.explanation: Do not cut back. Strong youth work – not 'soft' but with clear boundaries – prevents nuisance. This keeps neighborhoods livable and safe.

110328.explanation: More charging points are needed, but with balance. Electric drivers must be able to charge while there remains enough space for petrol or diesel cars.

110329.explanation: The residential streets are already 30 km/h. We want to keep the main roads at 50 km/h. That prevents shortcut traffic and keeps the city accessible without unnecessary delays.

110330.explanation: No extra permits. Driving is already expensive. Don’t make parking a cash cow: provide enough spaces and fair distribution.

110333.explanation: Price is important, but not everything. In youth care, quality definitely counts too. Cheap care that doesn’t help ultimately costs more. Choose effective and affordable.

110334.explanation: Nothing is truly free. In the end, it is all paid for with all our tax money.

110337.explanation: Increasing it is unwise. We want to attract visitors who spend money in our municipality. That sustains services without pricing us out of the market.

110338.explanation: Yes to De Spie and the southern part of the Auvergnepolder for high-quality business parks. This strengthens our local economy and creates jobs and opportunities, also for future generations.

110340.explanation: Unfair to our own residents. Local ties are important: those rooted here and from here must have a fair chance at a home.

110342.explanation: No solar panels on agricultural land. Space is scarce; first use roofs before sacrificing valuable farmland for inefficient solutions.

110344.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country. Swimming skills prevent accidents. Child safety is paramount; that’s why we support school swimming.

110347.explanation: The 30% rule puts the emphasis in the wrong place. We want more focus on affordable homes for middle incomes and seniors: that’s where the greatest need is and it increases financial support.

110348.explanation: We want housing that fits the surroundings. Sometimes that is a terraced house, sometimes a multi-story apartment building. That’s fine. But Bergen op Zoom is not Rotterdam.

110349.explanation: Agreed, because it causes nuisance and pressure on neighborhoods. Slumlords rent terraced homes to multiple migrant workers. Not good for them and not for the neighborhood.

110354.explanation: We prefer to prevent homelessness. Also to avoid nuisance. But if it happens, night shelters are better than wandering the city and sleeping in doorways and parks.

110356.explanation: Nice idea, but it costs buckets of money: a bridge too far. We’d rather spend that on real priorities for residents, such as safety and more affordable homes.

110357.explanation: Migrant workers have to live somewhere. Being populist against it doesn’t help. Prefer housing intended for them rather than displacing ordinary families from terraced houses.

110297.explanation: The CDA Bergen op Zoom believes that the quality of leaders must come first.

110298.explanation: First, you need to clearly know what you consider important topics. And then: how do you formulate the question or statement for a referendum? And: who judges if that question or statement is correct? The municipal council must first make careful agreements about this.

110302.explanation: The CDA Bergen op Zoom believes we should always support good plans. In any case, the national government should also contribute more.

110304.explanation: As long as safety is guaranteed: owners must regularly have their chimneys swept and not use protected wood types.

110306.explanation: If it can be organized safely, definitely do it. Perhaps also with the possibility to have a snack and drink. There are good examples abroad.

110307.explanation: You already pay for that now. There are sufficient options to have furniture etc. collected by St. Vincentius, De Kringloper. Companies also pick up various types of waste.

110310.explanation: This already largely happens because there are multiple schemes to support people. Exercise is actually good and activates people to participate in society.

110314.explanation: Monuments attract tourists and make the centers of our municipality so special.

110316.explanation: See above as well. Cameras increase the feeling of safety among our residents.

110318.explanation: This increases their ability to act in extreme cases.

110324.explanation: The CDA Bergen op Zoom believes that this gives people a more meaningful existence and more structure. This increases their chances of future employment.

110326.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain the current income threshold. However, we must do everything possible to reach residents who can make use of the arrangements.

110327.explanation: The number of young people requiring support is increasing. However, there needs to be a better assessment of whether the care provision should be organized differently. So less 'expensive' care.

110328.explanation: For example, in larger parking lots.

110329.explanation: On main roads, 50 km/h should remain the rule. Otherwise, you get much more cut-through traffic, which nobody wants.

110330.explanation: There is currently no necessity for that.

110333.explanation: Quality is at least as important.

110334.explanation: For students or people with low incomes, yes.

110337.explanation: First thoroughly investigate the effects: what benefits does it bring and does it affect the number of tourists?

110338.explanation: An important condition: clean business parks, so no polluting companies and no logistics warehouses that cause a lot of traffic nuisance.

110340.explanation: There are long waiting lists for our own residents.

110342.explanation: A few locations have been designated and that is sufficient. Our scarce farmland is needed to provide our own food in the future. Solar panels preferably on roofs.

110344.explanation: Who will use it? It is a huge organization for schools. First ask their opinion.

110347.explanation: We are already working on that now. Waiting lists for people with low incomes have been long enough.

110348.explanation: Provided they do not obstruct the view. But very tall apartment blocks are, due to safety measures, nowadays also relatively more expensive.

110349.explanation: CDA Bergen op Zoom is against profiteering landlords. But sometimes it may be necessary to help, for example, young people with a low income or migrant workers.

110354.explanation: There is currently one. However, the care for addicts should once be taken over by another municipality in the region.

110356.explanation: As it looks now, this will be a very expensive plan. For whom? So only if there is a sober and affordable plan.

110357.explanation: The question is: is this still possible? A legal procedure is currently underway.

110297.explanation: Quality is more important than gender.

110298.explanation: Use it sparingly, but it's good that the option exists.

110306.explanation: It should be possible, for example near the Boulevard. No obligation.

110314.explanation: Monuments are our history. It is important that they are preserved and cherished.

110316.explanation: Selective use of cameras, no big brother is watching you.

110318.explanation: A baton should be an option, with the condition that it comes with self-control training. Safety is primarily a police matter.

110324.explanation: Instead of having to provide a service according to ability, they must give something back to society.

110326.explanation: For example, up to 130% of the social assistance standard, but at least equal to the current 120%.

110329.explanation: Agreed in residential neighborhoods, but main roads can remain 50 km/h.

110330.explanation: We prefer fewer cars in the neighborhood and this can ensure fewer cars per household.

110334.explanation: These may also be more sustainable solutions.

110340.explanation: As a municipality, we do not allocate housing; housing corporations do. A (national) legal regulation requiring housing corporations to annually house a certain percentage or number of refugees with residence permits would provide a solution.

110348.explanation: With the right frameworks regarding quality and living environment.

110349.explanation: Renting out rooms can offer a solution to the current housing shortage.

110357.explanation: Block this location, but housing must still be realized for migrant workers.

110297.explanation: The best person should become alderman. The distinction between man and woman is therefore incorrect. If you want to make a distinction, it is better to choose more entrepreneurs in the college.

110298.explanation: With a broad referendum, no asylum seekers' center (AZC) would be established anywhere. A referendum is a democratic means to rein in the municipality. Too many decisions are now made against the will of citizens.

110302.explanation: If all of the Netherlands is fossil-free, this yields 0.0000036% climate benefit. Bergen op Zoom's contribution is even lower. So this is not productive. The municipality may welcome and possibly facilitate local initiatives but should not provide financial support.

110304.explanation: BVB wants everyone to have freedom of choice. Only in cases of major nuisance or danger to public health should the municipality intervene.

110306.explanation: BVB wants to maintain the freedom to light fireworks themselves. Additionally, a fireworks show is welcome.

110307.explanation: In the past, the collection of bulky waste was the norm. That should return. Household waste should also be collected weekly again. Money should once again be spent on facilities for citizens.

110310.explanation: BVB wants a small and decisive municipality. If we achieve that, there will be enough money left to give back to all citizens. They can then decide for themselves what to do with it.

110314.explanation: Heritage is an important part of the identity of Bergen op Zoom. We must cherish and preserve it for future generations. There are enough other expenses to cut back on. By making the municipal organization smaller and more efficient, we will have enough money left.

110316.explanation: There are sufficient other means to improve safety. BVB does not believe in more camera surveillance. We are increasingly moving towards a control society modeled after China. That is an unacceptable infringement on our freedom.

110318.explanation: Only the police have the right to use (controlled) force to maintain order. They are trained for this; enforcement officers (Boa's) are not suitable.

110324.explanation: BVB sees advantages in deploying social assistance recipients. However, people must truly want this themselves. Coercion is pointless. When people move from social assistance to work, they end up worse off due to losing benefits. Something must be done about this, but that solution must come from The Hague.

110326.explanation: BVB does not believe in poverty reduction but does believe in people's self-reliance. The problem is that when people move from social assistance to work, they end up worse off due to losing benefits. Something must be done about this, but that is The Hague's responsibility.

110327.explanation: Cutting back is not desirable. However, purchasing care can be scrutinized. Currently, too much money ends up with commercial care providers.

110328.explanation: There must remain room for electric cars, but expanding charging points is not on the agenda. The energy transition is proceeding extremely slowly. A fundamentally different course is needed, but that must be determined by The Hague.

110329.explanation: The current maximum speed is fine. There is no problem solved by lowering the maximum speed. The city center must remain lively. With 30 km/h, entrepreneurs and residents are only hindered.

110330.explanation: Freedom to park is the starting point. There is no reason to expand paid parking. That would only lead to higher costs. BVB actually wants a reduction of all taxes and levies. We want to finance this by making the municipality smaller and more efficient.

110333.explanation: Quality must be leading. If this can be done at a low price, then please, but not at the expense of quality. Currently, too much money ends up with commercial care providers.

110334.explanation: Poverty should not be fought, but prevented. We see no role for the municipality in distributing tampons and sanitary pads.

110337.explanation: Tourist tax only drives visitors away from the city. BVB wants to reduce all taxes and levies. We want to finance this by making the municipality smaller and more efficient.

110338.explanation: Bergen op Zoom has enormous potential due to its location between Rotterdam and Antwerp. With business parks, we can increase prosperity in our municipality. More purchasing power means more shops (less vacancy), thus a vital city center and good facilities.

110340.explanation: Everyone must wait his turn, refugee or not. BVB is the only party in Bergen op Zoom that has consistently opposed mass immigration, asylum seeker centers, and preferential treatment of asylum seekers over the past 4 years.

110342.explanation: Fertile farmland should be used for our food supply.

110344.explanation: Swimming skills among youth are extremely important. Parents are primarily responsible for this. The municipality can help but only on initiative and with funding from The Hague.

110347.explanation: Sometimes 30% social housing is simply not financially feasible. Social housing is good, but we cannot afford to impose restrictive requirements on housing construction.

110348.explanation: BVB does not want a new Lucerna flat. Medium-height buildings (3 to 4 floors) suit the municipality better. High-rise is an efficient use of scarce space. That is good against housing shortage but must fit the environment.

110349.explanation: Homeowners must be able to rent out rooms, but nuisance must be strictly tackled. Renting rooms helps against the housing shortage because more people live in one home.

110354.explanation: Care for those in need should again be distributed across the region. The nuisance in Bergen op Zoom is currently too high.

110356.explanation: BVB would like to preserve and, if necessary, restore the historical value of Bergen op Zoom. However, restoring water and a bridge at the Gevangenpoort is not a priority. There are more urgent matters to address first.

110357.explanation: Housing in Bergen op Zoom for labor migrants working in Rotterdam is ridiculous. BVB wants housing for our own residents. High-rise is completely undesirable. The project at Havendijk is not in the interest of neighbors nor the municipality.

110297.explanation: Candidates should be chosen based on knowledge and experience.

110298.explanation: With clear rules on how this is organized.

110302.explanation: There are already sufficient regulations for this. For example, we have conditions for monumental buildings.

110304.explanation: Fine dust from wood burning is heavily burdensome for people with lung problems. Also, children are increasingly developing lung-related conditions.

110306.explanation: If a fireworks ban applies to everyone, it must also apply to the municipality. Equal treatment for all.

110307.explanation: This must remain possible and we will make good agreements about it.

110310.explanation: And existing arrangements must become better known.

110314.explanation: We keep our existing monuments in good condition.

110316.explanation: Camera surveillance creates a false sense of security.

110318.explanation: Let's first organize this properly on a national level.

110324.explanation: Volunteering may never be mandatory. Moreover, work must pay to get out of the benefit situation.

110326.explanation: The costs of living and energy continue to rise. We aim for at least 130% of the benefit level.

110327.explanation: We actually see the benefits of well-organized youth work.

110328.explanation: We move along with developments. It must be well arranged that fully loaded vehicles free the parking spot for the next user.

110329.explanation: This is determined based on traffic safety and how a neighborhood is designed. Give people the opportunity to have a say.

110330.explanation: First, look at the parking pressure in the neighborhoods.

110334.explanation: Let it especially be part of poverty reduction.

110337.explanation: We want to be a tourist-friendly municipality.

110338.explanation: We must use space consciously. We do this in good consultation.

110340.explanation: Agreements have already been laid down for this and we implement them.

110342.explanation: We aim for more solar panels on roofs and non-commercial lands (e.g., old landfill sites).

110344.explanation: Every child leaves primary school with a swimming diploma.

110347.explanation: We maintain 30% social housing, 40% mid-range rental, and 30% (affordable) owner-occupied homes.

110348.explanation: This is determined by whether it is possible and in which locations.

110349.explanation: We combat subdividing homes into multiple units to profit financially. We assume good living quality. We allow splitting homes under good conditions and in consultation.

110354.explanation: Link this immediately to care so that people can improve their situation.

110356.explanation: Only if this is financially feasible within the plan and can be incorporated. It is not mandatory.

110357.explanation: Previous agreements with fairground operators are respected. We also focus on clear conditions. We consider releasing employment contracts and lease agreements. Homelessness among labor migrants must not be shifted to the municipality.

110297.explanation: SAMEN0164 chooses quality across all portfolios. We assess people based on their expertise, commitment, and proven ability, never on their gender. Equal opportunities are a given for us; what matters is what someone contributes to a strong and future-proof municipality.

110298.explanation: Democracy is stronger when residents can genuinely participate in decisions. That is why it is good that citizens have direct input on legislation that affects their lives. As a transparent and fair instrument, the referendum ensures that legislation is made with society.

110302.explanation: The municipality must facilitate resident initiatives for sustainable, locally generated energy. Residents who invest together in clean energy deserve recognition for this.

110304.explanation: In Bergen op Zoom, the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves must remain allowed, provided this is done with modern techniques for optimal smoke and fine dust capture. This way, we can preserve tradition and living comfort while protecting the health and environment of our residents.

110306.explanation: Fireworks on New Year's Eve is a tradition. Private fireworks cause serious safety risks every year and encourage undesirable behavior. Therefore, it is better to switch to a centrally organized fireworks show by the municipality.

110307.explanation: Collecting bulky waste at home does not have to be free, except for people who cannot bring it themselves due to physical or other limitations. This keeps the system fair and affordable.

110310.explanation: For children in families receiving social assistance, the municipality must fully cover the membership fees for sports clubs. Under clear socio-economic conditions, this support can also be extended to adults, so everyone can participate in sports and exercise.

110314.explanation: Monument care is an investment in our identity and quality of life. Heritage strengthens the appeal of our municipality, stimulates the local economy, and prevents costly restorations in the future. Therefore, cutting back is unwise and ultimately more expensive.

110316.explanation: Our municipality faces a growing challenge: the structural shortage of police and enforcement capacity makes it impossible to be physically present everywhere. This harms livability and safety. That is why we choose modern, efficient, targeted camera surveillance.

110318.explanation: Community officers keep our public spaces safe but increasingly face aggression, while they work with a conflict-avoidance approach. It is unacceptable that officers serving on our behalf can themselves be unprotected targets.

110324.explanation: Participation in social life is paramount here. With tailored support, every social assistance recipient should be enabled and encouraged to do so.

110326.explanation: Those who truly want to combat poverty provide generous and realistic policies that offer people a future perspective instead of accumulating problems. Additionally, a program must be initiated to support these residents so they can leave social assistance on their own strength.

110327.explanation: Cutting youth work is unwise and ultimately more costly (nuisance/youth care). Young people need safe places to release their energy, find guidance, and build their future. If we dismantle this, we lose connection with young people.

110328.explanation: The national government is promoting the use of more electric cars. The municipality must ensure sufficient, reliable, and strategically placed charging points in every neighborhood.

110329.explanation: Traffic safety is a basic requirement for a livable neighborhood. Lower speeds demonstrably lead to fewer accidents and injuries. We do emphasize strict enforcement of the speed limit for fatbikes and e-bikes.

110330.explanation: Parking permits may be necessary to keep neighborhoods livable, but they are not an end in themselves. Therefore, we only use them where parking nuisance occurs and where residents and businesses can no longer find parking. Tailored solutions considering the local situation are needed.

110333.explanation: Quality is our primary focus. Young people are at the start of their life path. If they do not receive the right help, they will pay the price later, as will society. We only choose providers who deliver proven good, effective, and safe care.

110334.explanation: Period poverty limits participation in social life. It leads to school absenteeism, social exclusion, and health risks—problems easily preventable in a prosperous country.

110337.explanation: Increasing the tourist tax pressures the viability of hotels and B&Bs. The sector is already under pressure due to increased VAT hikes. This is not a healthy course for our leisure economy.

110338.explanation: Developing new industrial parks only makes sense if we first develop our existing ones. We choose optimization over expansion. We primarily focus on future-oriented companies: manufacturing and biobased enterprises. They add value and employment.

110340.explanation: These homes are scarce. Therefore, we must allocate them fairly and transparently. Everyone must participate via the same admission list and under the same rules. This is necessary to maintain trust in the system. Residents who have waited for years deserve a fair process.

110342.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, not on fertile agricultural land. We do not choose to damage the soil quality of farmland. Moreover, it puts pressure on the space needed for food production. We choose solar panels on already built locations.

110344.explanation: School swimming is not a luxury but a basic requirement to let children grow up safely in a water-rich environment. Moreover, we live in a municipality deeply connected to the Oosterschelde.

110347.explanation: In new construction, a minimum of 30% social housing can only be realized in neighborhoods where the share of social rental is too low. We aim for balance, not concentration—a conscious step towards a balanced, livable city.

110348.explanation: High-rise buildings can make a valuable contribution to housing needs but may only be built if they do not impair the living environment and demonstrably fit into the existing surroundings. We stand for spatial choices that add quality and do not compromise character or livability.

110349.explanation: The municipality must be able to supervise living conditions.

110354.explanation: This is necessary to prevent people from being forced to wander around at night. It not only provides safety and peace but also the opportunity for real help. Without a place where people can settle, problems remain invisible and unsolvable. Essential to help someone move forward.

110356.explanation: This restores the historical connection between the city center and the harbor district, enriches the cityscape, improves water management, and acts as a natural speed reducer. An improvement in the layout that smartly combines history, safety, and the future.

110357.explanation: It is unacceptable to house 450 labor migrants here, almost all of whom work outside the municipality. Employment agency Covebo is the only agency housing people here. Only 70 of them can move out from the neighborhoods. Build for these people and additionally for the housing demand.

110297.explanation: It remains difficult but Bergs Collectief supports the intention.

110298.explanation: For general interests that evoke many emotions in society, we think it is excellent to consult the public, which makes it a bit less political.

110302.explanation: Sustainable initiatives are good, but not every project should automatically receive subsidies. The municipality must handle public money carefully and only support if demonstrably necessary and effective.

110304.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves is a personal choice. A general ban mainly affects residents who use them responsibly. Nuisance should be tackled specifically, not with a total ban.

110306.explanation: A central fireworks show ensures safety, oversight, and less nuisance. It offers a shared moment for the city and can limit private fireworks.

110307.explanation: Free collection sounds attractive but leads to higher costs for everyone. It is reasonable that users pay a contribution, with exceptions for people who really cannot bring waste themselves.

110310.explanation: Sports are important, but a fully paid membership by the municipality goes too far. Support can better be targeted and supplementary, not standard. The role of welfare benefits is a safety net for people without sufficient income to sustain themselves.

110314.explanation: In financially difficult times, choices must be made. Monument preservation must not come at the expense of basic provisions for residents.

110318.explanation: Enforcement must be de-escalating. Extra arming can increase tensions and does not fit the role of the public order officers.

110324.explanation: Participation strengthens self-esteem and increases the chance of returning to work, provided it is appropriate and voluntary where necessary.

110326.explanation: Help should remain focused on the lowest incomes. Further raising thresholds makes schemes more expensive and less targeted.

110327.explanation: Youth work prevents problems at a later age. Cutting back actually leads to more nuisance and higher costs later.

110328.explanation: Electric driving is growing. Sufficient charging points are needed to enable this transition.

110329.explanation: Lower speeds increase traffic safety and make residential neighborhoods calmer and safer for pedestrians and cyclists.

110330.explanation: Parking permits should serve a purpose and not become a cash cow.

110333.explanation: Quality must be paramount in youth care. Too much focus on price leads to worse care and ultimately higher social costs.

110334.explanation: In places where it is not visible due to shame.

110347.explanation: You have to determine it per situation, otherwise, you create ghettos.

110348.explanation: Partly solves housing shortage and requires little space.

110349.explanation: Renting out individual rooms often causes nuisance and displacement in neighborhoods. By retaining whole homes, livability is better maintained and homes are available for families.

110297.explanation: For the PVV, gender does not matter but quality, experience, and decisiveness. Administrators must be appointed based on suitability and the interests of residents, not on imposed quotas or symbolic politics.

110298.explanation: Residents of Bergen op Zoom should not only be able to give their opinion but also really be able to decide. The PVV wants binding referenda so that power lies with the residents and not with a ruling elite.

110302.explanation: Those who want to generate sustainable energy together must do so voluntarily and without public money. Municipal funds are for safety, care, and facilities for residents.

110304.explanation: The use of fireplaces, wood stoves, and barbecues should remain allowed. The PVV believes in freedom and common sense and thinks that residents should be able to work this out among themselves without patronizing bans.

110306.explanation: The PVV believes New Year’s Eve is a celebration for the residents themselves. The municipality does not need to organize an expensive fireworks spectacle with taxpayer money; that money should go to safety, care, and facilities for residents.

110307.explanation: Nothing is free. The PVV does want bulky waste collected every 14 days. By working more efficiently, costs can be lowered without additional burdens for residents and without polluting streets and neighborhoods.

110310.explanation: The PVV believes this discourages (more) work and increases dependency. Working must always pay more than benefits. Help must be targeted and temporary, with customization for real problem cases and with strict but fair controls.

110314.explanation: Monuments are part of Dutch identity and history. The PVV believes the municipality should not cut back on their preservation, but must be frugal and wise with expenses, avoiding unnecessary costs or prestige projects.

110316.explanation: The PVV wants more camera surveillance in Bergen op Zoom to tackle crime and nuisance firmly. It increases safety and the sense of security of residents and helps police and enforcement in their work. Safety always comes first.

110318.explanation: The PVV wants first and foremost more police on the streets. In addition, well-trained and educated public order officers must be able to do their work safely. A baton is a necessary tool amid increasing aggression and violence.

110324.explanation: Nationally, over half of social assistance goes to non-Western immigrants. A benefit is not an unconditional right. Those who can work must give something back. A mandatory counter-performance is necessary to stop abuse and dependency.

110326.explanation: The PVV opposes the continuous extension of income limits. That increases dependency and discourages (more) work. Help must be targeted and temporary, with customization for real problem cases. Working must always pay more than benefits.

110327.explanation: The PVV is against rewarding misbehavior. Young people who misbehave must be tackled, not pampered with community centers and projects. Youth work must focus on order, discipline, and safety, not on looking away or rewarding.

110328.explanation: The PVV opposes further sacrificing parking spaces for electric cars. Parking pressure must not increase due to climate policy. The installation of charging points is not the municipality's task but that of the market. Private individuals should be given space for their own solutions, such as gutters.

110329.explanation: The PVV is against a general reduction to 30 km/h. That is patronizing and harms traffic flow. In many places, driving safely at 50 km/h or faster is possible, with tailored measures where necessary.

110330.explanation: The PVV opposes expanding parking permits. That drives up costs for residents and increases parking pressure. The municipality should ensure there are enough parking spaces so that parking in one’s own neighborhood remains affordable and accessible.

110333.explanation: The PVV believes that quality and effectiveness should be prioritized in youth care, not just price. At the same time, bureaucracy must be tackled and abuse combated. Money should go to help for children, not paperwork and failing organizations.

110334.explanation: The PVV is against symbolic policies and handing out free products with taxpayer money. Menstrual products are personal expenses. Municipal funds should go to core tasks such as safety, care, and targeted help for people truly in difficulty.

110337.explanation: Bergen op Zoom must become a vibrant city again. Raising the tourist tax drives visitors away and harms the hospitality industry and entrepreneurs. The municipality should rather cut back on left-wing hobbies than on tourism and liveliness.

110338.explanation: More business parks are only considered if existing sites are truly full. New companies must demonstrably add value to Bergen op Zoom and provide jobs for local staff. Companies primarily relying on labor migrants are not welcome.

110340.explanation: The PVV absolutely opposes any priority for status holders. Bergen op Zoom cannot handle further inflows and concentrations. Social housing is for local residents. Therefore, the PVV also wants a strict language requirement for rental homes: at least Dutch at B1 level.

110342.explanation: The PVV opposes solar panels on agricultural land. That land is needed for food production. We are proud of our farmers and want to protect their land. The PVV also wants to preserve the coastal fishing as part of our local tradition.

110344.explanation: The PVV considers school swimming essential for child safety. Every child must learn to swim. The municipality should finance this by cutting unnecessary left-wing hobbies and expensive prestige projects, not basic services that save lives.

110347.explanation: The PVV primarily wants to build affordable owner-occupied homes. Too much additional social housing increases problems and leads to ghettoization and Islamic enclaves. Housing corporations will have strict performance agreements, and access to social housing will be tied to mastering Dutch at B1 level.

110348.explanation: High-rise buildings can be part of the solution to the housing shortage, but the PVV does not want new ghettos or concentrations of ethnic minorities. Neighborhoods must remain safe and manageable. For rental homes, the PVV wants to introduce a language requirement: at least Dutch at B1 level.

110349.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, room rentals can temporarily be a solution for Dutch people. Not for labor migrants. It must not cause nuisance; problems must be addressed immediately. The real solution is building more homes.

110354.explanation: The PVV supports a night shelter but with strict conditions. The shelter is temporary, intended for local residents only, and must not cause any nuisance. Foreign homeless people do not belong here and should be deported.

110356.explanation: The PVV opposes this expensive prestige project. Restoring water and building a bridge at the Gevangenpoort costs a lot and delivers little for residents. That money can be better spent on safety, accessibility, and real city problems.

110357.explanation: The PVV does not want a mega labor migrant complex. The claim that this frees up housing for locals is misleading: it actually attracts more labor migrants and increases pressure and nuisance. Priority must be housing for our own young people and families.

statements.title: The municipality must carry out the plan to restore the water and the bridge at the Gevangenpoort in Bergen op Zoom.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: More high-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Bergen op Zoom.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted in Bergen op Zoom.

titleParts.text: In Bergen op Zoom moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
More CCTV surveillance must be installed in Bergen op Zoom.

titleParts.text: In Bergen op Zoom moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Bergen op Zoom must be equipped with a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Bergen op Zoom controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The residents of Bergen op Zoom must be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bergen op Zoom moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Bergen op Zoom must be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Bergen op Zoom moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: A night shelter for the homeless must be established in Bergen op Zoom.
More business parks must be developed in Bergen op Zoom.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Bergen op Zoom must introduce residential parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Bergen op Zoom moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the construction of accommodation for labor migrants on Havendijk.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de bouw van huisvesting voor

titleParts.glossary: A person who comes to the Netherlands from another country temporarily to work.
